evidence: OMS is early access and launching in phases; exact platform pricing is not public.
Public Polygon positioning includes low onchain settlement cost signals; use network cost as a public signal, not full OMS price.
Programmable wallet pricing is based on monthly active wallets with a free allowance and tiered MAW pricing.
Embedded wallet pricing includes a free wallet-operation allowance followed by public per-operation pricing.
Wallet pricing includes a free wallet allowance and wallet overage pricing; payments are tiered/custom.
Dynamic publishes a free tier and paid growth plan.
Fireblocks publishes starter and custom annual pricing signals.
Turnkey exposes a small free signature allowance and paid usage model.
Bridge discloses gas pass-through, partner charges, and FX spread up to 1%.
BVNK material cites stablecoin payment provider fee ranges and custom enterprise pricing.
Issuer fee schedules publish selected stablecoin pass-through fees.
CCIP publishes gas and network fee billing components.
LayerZero execution fees depend on executor, gas, and message configuration.
Relayer costs depend on delivery, gas, and route behavior.
Across documents dynamic fees including relayer and LP components.
MoonPay publishes direct-fee maximums and product-specific pricing disclosures.
Ramp publishes bank and card fee ranges by region/product.
Coinbase describes onramp product and USDC route offers; exact partner terms vary.
Banxa exposes partner/onramp pricing information with route-dependent fees.
Onramper publishes monthly plan pricing for its aggregator.
AvaCloud publishes monthly plan levels for testnet, mainnet, and pro chain deployments.
OP Stack is positioned as open-source infrastructure; service and ops costs vary.
Arbitrum Orbit provides chain deployment framework; commercial model depends on deployment path.
Alchemy Rollups is offered as a rollup launch platform with custom/demo-led pricing.
Wise publishes business pricing and upfront fee calculators.
Airwallex publishes plan and product pricing signals, with some features custom.
Nium markets 190+ payout countries and 100+ real-time corridors; pricing is sales-led.
Polygon Labs announced acquisition of Coinme (Jan 2026); Coinme operates as licensed US on/off-ramp with 48-state MTL and 50K+ retail locations; B2B pricing custom.

source: Sequence Knowledge Base + Polygon Labs acquisition
evidence: Free tier covers 0–499 MAU and 50K signatures; Core $299/mo to 2.5K MAU; enterprise per-signature pricing. Acquired by Polygon Labs Jan 2026 as part of OMS.
Sequence Trails intents engine processed 10M+ transactions in under 2 months with reported 2x conversion lift vs. non-Trails wallets.
Agglayer connection is free for CDK chains; fees usage-based and payable in any token; pessimistic-proof security model.
Morpho-powered vaults convert idle bridged TVL into chain-level revenue; Katana deployment generated $2M+ revenue from $230M deposited.
Open-source ZK identity protocol; no per-verification fees; credentials marketplace model.
Open-source ZK credentials; reusable across apps and chains.
Average transaction cost $0.001–0.002; 5-second finality after Polygon fast-finality upgrade; 3,200+ TPS reported.
ZK-rollup transaction cost roughly 7-15x Polygon PoS, in the ~$0.015 range; finality under 2.5 seconds.
OP Stack rollup with ~$0.09 average transaction cost; 2-second L2 confirmation, 7-day L1 challenge window.
Optimistic rollup, ~$0.09 transaction cost, TimeBoost MEV auction, 7-day challenge window.
OP Stack Superchain members owe 2.5% of revenue OR 15% of profit (whichever greater) to OP Collective.
Base fee $0.0005–0.0018 + priority fees; ~480ms finality; 5,500 actual TPS reported.
Base fee 100 stroops per operation; transactions typically under $0.001.
USDT transfers on Tron typically cost $1.87–3.74 in energy/bandwidth equivalent; ~75% of global USDT transfer volume.
May 2026 average fees historically low (sub-cent for simple transfers); variable under load.
Disclosed FX spread up to 1% (100 bps); engine modeled at 70 bps mid-point per re-review.
Token transfers typically 0.063–0.07% (~6–7 bps) plus destination gas; engine adjusted from 3 to 6.
Token transfers ~0.10% (10 bps) + message fee + destination gas; engine adjusted from 5 to 10 bps.
Basic $149/mo + $1.35/verification; Compliance $299/mo + $1.85/verification. Engine updated to model per-verification cost on top of the platform fee.
Essential plan from $250/mo + per-verification; startup program offers 500 free verifications/mo for eligible companies.
Essential $49/mo + $0.80–3.00 per verification; Plus $99/mo; Premium $209/mo + per-check.
Quote-only; industry benchmarks place enterprise contracts around $60K/yr median with 20–30% bundled discounts.
Custom pricing; benchmarks place platform contracts near $145K/yr median.
Virtual cards $0.10; physical cards $3.50; no monthly minimum; interchange revenue share available with Treasury.
Unit shares 70% of net interchange to fintech program; custom monthly minimums (default $0).
Marqeta captures 100% of interchange and shares with clients; enterprise minimums and per-transaction processing fees apply.
Nationally-chartered bank; 100% interchange to program manager before bank fees; ~$0.50 ACH and ~$5 wire.
ERC-4626 vaults curated by Gauntlet/Steakhouse and others; 10–15% performance fee typical (curator-dependent).
Reserve factor (protocol cut of borrow interest) typically ~10%; USDC/USDT supply APY 3–5% recent.
5% protocol fee on yield accrued; $3.5B TVL recent.
yVault V2/V3 charges 20% performance fee + 2% management fee.
Free tier up to 499 MAU; Core $299/mo to 2.5K MAU; enterprise per-signature pricing. Acquired by Stripe/Bridge June 2025.
Protocol contracts free to deploy and use (gas only); enterprise services and audited custom integrations vary.
USDC mint/redemption free for Circle Mint customers; CCTP cross-chain transfer free (gas only).
Stripe stablecoin payments public signal around 1.5% per transaction.
Regulated stablecoin issuer; white-label issuance custom; mint/redeem free for primary partners.
Off-ramp fees approximately 1%; on-ramp fees route-dependent (1–3% bank, higher for cards).
Aggregator typically takes 0.25% default + underlying route fees.
KYT platform pricing custom; enterprise contracts in the $50K–150K/yr range typical based on volume.
KYT platform pricing custom; enterprise contracts similar range to Chainalysis.
